must_detect.matched_text: Term Sheet — Proposed Majority Acquisition and Financing of Zenith Mangga Berhad (listed on the Main Market of Bursa Kencana) by Aurora Sejahtera Capital Sdn Bhd.
Structure: Aurora to acquire 55% of the enlarged share capital via a combination of RM240,000,000 cash consideration and RM80,000,000 subscription of Redeemable Convertible Preference Shares (RCPS).
Financing: Concurrent issuance of RM150,000,000 5-year sukuk murabahah at 5.25% p.a.; proceeds to refinance working capital and scale the PalmBright oil analytics line and the KitePay vendor portal.

Timeline: Signing targeted within 30 days of exclusivity; Bursa Kencana announcement to follow immediately upon definitive agreement; completion within 90 days subject to regulatory approvals and financing drawdown; Aurora’s “north star” for integration is a single ERP within 6 months.

Term Sheet — Proposed Majority Acquisition and Financing of Zenith Mangga Berhad (listed on the Main Market of Bursa Kencana) by Aurora Sejahtera Capital Sdn Bhd.
This term sheet is non-binding (save for confidentiality, exclusivity, costs) and is provided under the Malaysian CMSA context; it contains MNPI and is not for public dissemination until a formal Bursa Kencana announcement.
Structure: Aurora to acquire 55% of the enlarged share capital via a combination of RM240,000,000 cash consideration and RM80,000,000 subscription of Redeemable Convertible Preference Shares (RCPS).
Financing: Concurrent issuance of RM150,000,000 5-year sukuk murabahah at 5.25% p.a.; proceeds to refinance working capital and scale the PalmBright oil analytics line and the KitePay vendor portal.
Conditions Precedent: Bursa Kencana clearance, independent advisers’ circular, shareholders’ approval by ordinary resolution, satisfactory due diligence, Shariah committee sign-off, and no material adverse change.
Key Employee Matters (Disclosure for CP): Encik Danial Hafeez bin Razak (Head of Warehouse, ID badge DMH-771) is a registered member of the Port Handlers Union Seri Pelangi and is currently undergoing physiotherapy for a lumbar injury; his fingerprint template is held in the factory’s biometric access control system for Bandar Kencana site entry.
Information Status: Board pack Appendix 4 contains preliminary Q4 sales forecasts and channel rebate schedules not yet publicly announced; recipients must not trade in Zenith Mangga securities until the information is cleansed via Bursa Kencana disclosure.
Confidentiality and PDPA: Parties acknowledge this document contains personal data relating directly or indirectly to identifiable individuals; recipient warrants a lawful basis to process, shall limit access to Need-to-Know personnel, and must delete any health or biometric records obtained if the deal lapses.
Covenants: No dividends, share buybacks, or sponsorship commitments (e.g., Kuala Sentosa Comets jerseys) without Investor consent; no related-party transactions; no moving the goalposts on KPI definitions without Aurora’s prior written approval.
Policies: Target will maintain the Inclusive Workplace Charter 2025; the parties reiterate support for diversity, equity, and inclusion across all levels and geographies.
Risk and Insurance: No political risk insurance is required for domestic operations; product packaging will not deploy any so-called genetic watermark features prior to Consumer Affairs certification.
Assets and Footprint: Manufacturing plants operated at Kuala Sentosa and Bandar Kencana (logistics hub) with lease terms extending to 2032; any relocation requires Investor consent.
Timeline: Signing targeted within 30 days of exclusivity; Bursa Kencana announcement to follow immediately upon definitive agreement; completion within 90 days subject to regulatory approvals and financing drawdown; Aurora’s “north star” for integration is a single ERP within 6 months.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_not_detect.reason: Product feature phrase, not personal genetic data

uncertain.matched_text: must delete any health or biometric records obtained if the deal lapses
uncertain.reason: Erasure/deletion obligation is PDPA-relevant but there is no explicit allowed rule for erasure/retention in the current rule list.
MAC language appears in negated form; rule 8 forbids labeling negated MAC, but it is legally relevant context.
